name: cosmoteer-language-server
description: A complete language server for Cosmoteer rules
author: Cosmoteer Modding Tools

cosmoteerLSPRules.maxNumberOfProblems.description: Controls the maximum number of problems produced by the server.

cosmoteerLSPRules.trace.server.description: Traces the communication between VS Code and the language server.

cosmoteerLSPRules.cosmoteerPath.description: The path to the Cosmoteer installation. If not set, the extension cannot validate .rules files correctly. Example: C:/Program Files (x86)/Steam/steamapps/common/Cosmoteer

cosmoteerLSPRules.ignorePaths.description: Paths to ignore when validating references. Example: ["SW_"]

cosmoteerLSPRules.diagnostics.validateWh.description: Validate every .rules file in the open workspace folder(s), not just the files open in the editor. Uses more memory and CPU on large projects.

enumDescriptions: Validate every .rules file under the workspace folder(s).
Validate only the files reachable from the mod.rules manifest: its action sources, their includes and inheritance, and the strings folder. Files open in the editor always validate.
cosmoteerLSPRules.diagnostics.workspaceV.description: Which files the whole-workspace validation pass covers (only relevant when 'validateWholeWorkspace' is on).

cosmoteerLSPRules.diagnostics.validateCo.description: Flag a component ID reference (e.g. 'OperationalToggle = IsOperational') whose id names no component in the part, its inherited bases, or its include-valued components blocks. Runs only once the game install is indexed.

cosmoteerLSPRules.diagnostics.validateCr.description: Flag a cross-file ID reference (a GUI toggle/color/targeter/trigger id) whose id names no declaration of that kind in the project or the game data. Runs only once the game install is indexed.

cosmoteerLSPRules.diagnostics.validateRe.description: Flag a group that is missing a schema-required field. Fields supplied through the inheritance chain count as present.

cosmoteerLSPRules.diagnostics.validateSh.description: Flag an inline shader constant a material sets (e.g. '_hotColor = ...') that the referenced .shader declares no uniform for, or whose value does not match the uniform's type. Runs only when the shader file resolves on disk.

Run diagnostics on .shader files: an #include whose target does not exist, a '_'-prefixed uniform that no file in the include chain declares, and a call to a function that is neither an HLSL intrinsic nor defined in scope. The undeclared-symbol checks run only when the whole include chain is readable.

cosmoteerLSPRules.diagnostics.validateLo.description: Flag a localization key (e.g. 'NameKey = "Parts/Foo"') that no language strings file declares. Only literal key paths are checked and matching is case-insensitive, like the game. Runs only once the game install is indexed. Offers a 'did you mean' fix and an insert-into-your-strings-files fix.

cosmoteerLSPRules.diagnostics.validateRe.description: Hint at a ',' or ';' separator that a line break already makes redundant. The game ends every field or list element at a line break, so separators are only needed between entries on the same line. Shown as an editor hint, not in the Problems panel.

cosmoteerLSPRules.rename.allowEditingVan.description: Allow Rename to edit files inside the Cosmoteer game Data install. When off, a rename only edits files in your workspace.

cosmoteerLSPRules.associateShaderFiles.description: Open .shader files with the Cosmoteer Shader language (HLSL) even when another extension, such as the built-in Unity ShaderLab support, also claims the .shader extension. Only reassigns files opened as ShaderLab or plain text, never a language you chose yourself.

cosmoteerLSPRules.formatting.enabled.description: Enable document formatting (Format Document) for .rules and .shader files. Formatting changes whitespace only; if the result would not lex to the identical token stream, the file is left unchanged.

cosmoteerLSPRules.formatting.formatOnSav.description: Format .rules and .shader files before every save, independent of the editor's own 'editor.formatOnSave'. On-save formatting indents with tabs.

scripts.package: npm run check-types && npm run lint && node esbuild.mjs --production
scripts.postinstall: cd client && npm install && cd ../server && npm install && cd ..
